summary: PR #2751 improves the match for `gmMainLib_8015DBF4` in `src/melee/gm/gmmain_lib.c` by restructuring nametag-adjustment macros around separate load/store expressions and preserving local pointer lifetimes for repeated VsModeData mode-record loops. The PR reports no intended behavior change and includes build plus objdiff evidence showing the function improved from about 59.65% to 94.40% with exact 3724/3724-byte size.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; updates `gmMainLib_8015DBF4` nametag/mode-record adjustment logic and local lifetime shaping.

what_changed.text: Added locals `struct gmm_x0* load_gmm`, `struct gmm_x0_528_t* config`, and `u8* ptr` inside `gmMainLib_8015DBF4`.
Replaced the single-argument `ADJ_VMD(vmd)` macro with separate forms: `ADJ_VMD(load_vmd_expr, store_vmd_expr)` and `ADJ_VMD_SINGLE(vmd_expr)`.
Added nametag adjustment helper macros that separate load and store fields or reuse a preloaded value: `ADJ_NAMETAG_PAIR`, `ADJ_NAMETAG_STANDALONE_PAIR`, and `ADJ_NAMETAG_PRELOADED`.
Changed repeated mode-record calls from direct `gmMainLib_804D3EE0->...` expressions to `load_gmm->...` expressions while preserving paired load/store operands.
Introduced a preloaded `val` and `config` pointer path for `unk_51C.x4`, and one standalone byte access through `*((u8*) config + 0x18)` for the former `unk_530.x4` adjustment.

smart_moves.text: Kept the repeated player nametag adjustment as natural `for (j = 0; j < 6; j++)` loops instead of unrolling the mode-record updates.
Used narrow local macros to express repeated nametag adjustment variants while changing load/store expression shape for codegen.
Documented verifier evidence in the PR body: targeted ninja build plus objdiff score/size report.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For `gmMainLib_8015DBF4`-style repeated mode-record loops, matching may depend on preserving distinct load and store expressions even when they refer to the same logical VsModeData record.
Local pointer lifetimes can be a meaningful matching lever in this function; `load_gmm`, `config`, `ptr`, and preloaded `val` are used to shape the generated code for nametag updates.

naming_conventions.name: load_gmm
naming_conventions.lesson: Name distinguishes the expression used for loads from paired store expressions in mode-record loops.

naming_conventions.name: config
naming_conventions.lesson: Used for a `gmm_x0_528_t` substructure selected from `load_gmm->unk_51C`.

naming_conventions.name: ADJ_NAMETAG_PAIR / ADJ_NAMETAG_STANDALONE_PAIR / ADJ_NAMETAG_PRELOADED
naming_conventions.lesson: Macro names encode whether a nametag adjustment uses paired load/store fields, a standalone loaded value, or a preloaded value.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Preserve separate load/store expressions in macros instead of collapsing to a single lvalue expression.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.reported_result: `gmMainLib_8015DBF4` improved from 59.6541% to 94.402794%; bot report shows 59.65% to 94.40%.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Preserve or introduce local pointer lifetimes (`load_gmm`, `config`, `ptr`) and a preloaded byte value (`val`) to influence codegen in the mode-record loops.
Keep the repeated player adjustment as a `for (j = 0; j < 6; j++)` loop while varying expression shape through macro parameters.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.reported_result: Part of the diff associated with the reported match improvement.

notes: Changed-file evidence is complete for one source file and the diff excerpt contains the relevant function hunk.
The PR body explicitly states the intended matching tactic and no-behavior-change intent.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Separate load/store expressions helped `gmMainLib_8015DBF4` mode-record loop matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2751, `gmMainLib_8015DBF4` improved from about 59.65% to 94.40% after refactoring repeated VsModeData nametag-adjustment loops to preserve separate load and store expressions and local pointer lifetimes. This is supported for the `gmmain_lib` function, not as a global rule.

source_update_candidates.title: `gmmain_lib.c` mode-record matching may be sensitive to load/store expression identity
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2751 suggests that in `src/melee/gm/gmmain_lib.c`, especially `gmMainLib_8015DBF4`, repeated mode-record nametag adjustment loops may require preserving distinct load and store expression forms and local pointer lifetimes to improve matching.
source_update_candidates.reason: Path-scoped note could help future work in the same function avoid collapsing expression shapes that affect codegen.

source_update_candidates.title: Investigate typed field for `gmm_x0_528_t` byte at offset 0x18
source_update_candidates.text: The diff changes one access to `ADJ_NAMETAG_STANDALONE_PAIR(*((u8*) config + 0x18), *((u8*) config + 0x18))`. Curator may want to check whether `gmm_x0_528_t` has or should have a typed field for this byte before treating the raw pointer arithmetic as path-local style.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR evidence shows raw byte pointer arithmetic retained for matching, but does not prove whether a real field was unavailable.

rejection_notes.text: Do not generalize this PR into a project-wide rule that all equivalent load/store expressions should be split; the evidence is specific to `gmMainLib_8015DBF4` and its mode-record loops.
Do not treat raw byte pointer arithmetic at `config + 0x18` as an accepted style from this PR alone; loaded evidence does not establish the field/type recovery status.
